description_long: This pastry shop is housed in a charming, multi-story building with a classic European architectural style, featuring cream and beige facades, tall windows with decorative frames, and a mansard roof with multiple chimneys. The ground floor showcases two large awnings in orange and white stripes, suggesting the presence of a storefront or café area, and a central arched passageway connecting the two main building sections. The exterior is adorned with brickwork at the base and subtle decorative moldings above the windows. Street lamps, bollards, and a small sidewalk area enhance the urban setting, while a mural or painted sign is visible on one side of the building. The overall appearance is inviting and suggests a well-established pastry shop in a bustling city neighborhood.
description: A multi-story European-style pastry shop with striped awnings, tall windows, and a decorative facade, set in an urban streetscape with lamps and sidewalk details.
description_view_0: Side view showing a large mural or painted sign, two upper windows, and brickwork at the base.
description_view_1: Front view with two striped awnings, tall windows, a central arched passageway, and street lamps.
description_view_2: Rear side view highlighting the plain facade with repetitive window patterns and brick base.
description_view_3: Back view showing the arched passageway, multiple chimneys, and the rear of both building sections.
attribute_storefront_signage: There is a large mural or painted sign on the side wall, featuring a scenic or illustrative image, but no clear text or logo is visible. The awnings themselves are striped in orange and white, adding a decorative and recognizable element to the storefront.
attribute_architectural_style: The architectural style is reminiscent of late 19th to early 20th-century Parisian or Haussmannian design, characterized by mansard roofs, tall windows with decorative frames, brick and stone facades, and ornamental moldings.
